user_query:
I need a small, reliable scheduled job that helps me track official guidance relevant to soil management and water quality in Africa. Please create a script-based workflow that reads the provided configuration and, on a daily schedule, fetches and parses one relevant web page per source, then logs its status.

Inputs
- Config: input/config.yaml (provided)

Requirements
1) Implement a single runnable script at scripts/fetch_official_pages (you may choose the extension and language) that reads input/config.yaml.
2) For each source in the config:
   - Download the HTML for one page on the specified domain that best matches the source's title_hint. If you cannot reliably resolve a page from the hint, fall back to the domain root page.
   - Save the raw HTML to data/raw/{source_id}_YYYYMMDD.html (e.g., data/raw/fao_soils_20260115.html). Ensure the file is non-empty.
   - Compute a SHA-256 of the saved HTML and keep it for the results row.
   - Extract page title (if available), all anchor links, and headings whose text contains any of the source’s keywords (case-insensitive). Record matches.
3) Write a per-run results CSV at data/results/extracted_YYYYMMDD.csv with header and rows using these columns exactly:
   - source_id
   - fetched_at_iso (ISO-8601 timestamp for the fetch)
   - page_title
   - match_type ("link" or "heading")
   - match_keyword (the keyword that matched)
   - link_text (empty for heading matches)
   - link_href (absolute URL; empty for heading matches)
   - html_sha256
   Create or append the same rows to data/results/extracted_all.csv (include a single header row if creating the file; do not duplicate headers on append).
4) Create a machine-readable status summary at data/status/status.json containing exactly:
   {
     "schedule_spec": "<from config>",
     "last_run_iso": "<ISO timestamp>",
     "last_exit_code": <integer>,
     "stderr_tail": "<last 20 lines of stderr or null if none>",
     "stats": {"pages_fetched": <int>, "matches_found": <int>, "bytes_total": <int>}
   }
   - Derive last_exit_code and stderr_tail from the actual command’s execution (analyze the command’s output and any error messages). Do not fabricate values.
5) Create a cron specification file at schedule/cron.txt with a single line that runs the script using the schedule_spec from the config, with stdout and stderr redirected to logs/cron.out and logs/cron.err in the project root (ensure the paths exist or are created by your script). The cron entry can assume the script is invoked from the project root.
6) Run the script once now using a live Internet connection (do not hardcode direct URLs in the config or this request). After the run completes, ensure the following exist:
   - data/raw/{source_id}_YYYYMMDD.html (one per source)
   - data/results/extracted_YYYYMMDD.csv (with header even if no matches were found)
   - data/results/extracted_all.csv (header only is acceptable if first run)
   - data/status/status.json populated from the actual run’s exit code and stderr
   - schedule/cron.txt containing the cron line
   - logs/last_run.log or equivalent run log that your script uses to compute stderr_tail

Notes
- Use the organizations and hints from the config file to identify target pages (e.g., by resolving a page whose title contains the hint). If that fails, fall back to the domain root; record whatever you fetched.
- Internet use is required to download current HTML from the official domains. Extracted results may legitimately contain zero matches; that is acceptable provided the files, headers, and status fields are present.
- Please avoid direct URLs in your code derived from this request; it’s fine if your script discovers and uses URLs at runtime.

Deliverables are the created files under data/, schedule/, logs/, and scripts/ as specified above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
